ontology.analysis_summary: The Sejong Science Fellowship policy creates a 5-year (3+2) research grant program for early-career postdoctoral researchers in South Korea, administered by the Ministry of Science and ICT and the National Research Foundation. It targets non-tenure-track domestic researchers within 7 years of PhD or under age 39, providing annual funding of ~130M KRW. The policy includes eligibility boundaries, a dedicated evaluation panel, stage-gate evaluation at year 3, mobility allowances, and a 30% regional allocation quota. The ontology captures key actors (postdoc, host institution, ministry, NRF, evaluation panel), resources (research grant), information (eligibility criteria, research proposal), processes (stage evaluation), and scope rules (regional quota) to support stakeholder-centered impact simulation.

nodes.rationale: Central beneficiary actor; occupies the fellow position and drives the policy's impact simulation.

source_spans.text: Postdoctoral researchers and other young scientists are core research personnel who have difficulty conducting independent research due to low salary and research funding levels.
Postdoctoral researchers within 7 years of PhD or under 39, who are not full-time faculty, are eligible; foreign nationals cannot apply.
Domestic nationals are supported during the initial three years; PhD candidates who will obtain degree before research start date may apply.
Before applying, the researcher must complete consultation with the host institution on employment date, salary, and treatment.
Postdoctoral researchers are encouraged to move to a lab other than their PhD advisor's lab to strengthen creative research capabilities.
